Content: [Buzzing noise] [Schafer laughing]
Content_zh: [嗡嗡聲] [Schafer 笑聲]

Content: Schafer: Oh my god.
Content_zh: Schafer：天啊。

Content: Schafer: There you go.
Content_zh: Schafer：這樣就對了。

Content: Schafer: I forgot to put my beard on this morning, you're right.
Content_zh: Schafer：你說得對，我今天早上忘了戴鬍子。

Content: I left that beard at home.
Content_zh: 我把那副鬍子留在家裡了。

Content: People are going to know it's a fake beard.
Content_zh: 大家會發現那是假鬍子。

Content: Rice: I think that it's also just important to keep in mind that there's like other impacts besides just funding the game and that
Content_zh: Rice：我覺得也要記得，除了資助遊戲之外，這還有其他影響。

Content: like if we now go get a publisher for this game, then it's suddenly like voids everything we've said about the project
Content_zh: 像是如果我們現在去替這款遊戲找發行商，就等於突然推翻我們之前對這專案說過的一切。

Content: and everything we're trying to do.
Content_zh: 還有我們想做的所有事。

Content: Schafer: Where did we leave off?
Content_zh: Schafer：我們上次說到哪裡？

Content: Oh, that's right.
Content_zh: 喔，對了。

Content: Game was too big, company's going to close down, didn't have any money and Cloud Colony wasn't done.
Content_zh: 遊戲太大、公司快倒、沒錢了，而且雲端殖民地還沒做完。

Content: Bailey: Like because right now, you know, it looks like the plane's kind of diving down a little bit
Content_zh: Bailey：因為現在看起來，那架飛機有點往下俯衝。

Content: and we just need to, we have time to pull up.
Content_zh: 我們只要……我們還有時間把它拉起來。

Content: Schafer: What does the plane diving down stand for?
Content_zh: Schafer：飛機往下俯衝代表什麼？

Content: Bailey: That stands for Schafer: Us crashing?
Content_zh: Bailey：代表……Schafer：我們要墜毀？

Content: Schafer: The whole company crashing into the ground and burning? Bailey: [laughing] Rice: A lot actually.
Content_zh: Schafer：整間公司撞到地上燒起來？Bailey：[笑聲] Rice：其實很多事。

Content: Well, I mean right after that episode aired, yeah, right after that episode aired we started Amnesia Fortnight
Content_zh: 嗯，我是說那集播出之後，對，就在那集播出後，我們開始做 Amnesia Fortnight。

Content: so the team kind of took a break from everything for a little bit there.
Content_zh: 所以團隊暫時從所有事情裡抽離，休息了一下。

Content: Schafer: Amnesia Fortnight is coming up, people.
Content_zh: Schafer：各位，Amnesia Fortnight 要來了。

Content: And you forget what we were working on for two weeks, that's where the name comes from.
Content_zh: 接下來兩週你會忘掉原本在做什麼，名字就是這樣來的。

Content: We split the company into small groups, sometimes four or five groups.
Content_zh: 我們把公司分成幾個小組，有時候是四、五組。

Content: And each group has to make a game in two weeks.
Content_zh: 每組都要在兩週內做出一款遊戲。

Content: Rice: I think ultimately that was an amazing process and people had a great time and just came back really energized after that.
Content_zh: Rice：我覺得整體來說那是很棒的過程，大家玩得很開心，回來之後也充滿幹勁。

Content: And a lot of progress has already been made in this month since then.
Content_zh: 從那之後這一個月，已經有很多進展。

Content: Things are definitely moving along quicker and we're starting to address a lot of potential ways to help make production go smoothly
Content_zh: 事情確實推進得更快了，我們也開始處理很多可能讓製作更順的方法。

Content: and on a lower budget than it's looking like it could.
Content_zh: 而且預算也要比原本看起來更低。

Content: Schafer: There's scope and then there's money, then there's efficiencies.
Content_zh: Schafer：有範圍問題，有錢的問題，也有效率的問題。

Content: And I've never seen this solved any other way than all of the above.
Content_zh: 我從沒看過這種事能靠其中一項解決，一定是全部都要做。

Content: Stamos: Yeah. Schafer: You know what I mean?
Content_zh: Stamos：對。Schafer：你懂我的意思吧？

Content: Some scope reduction, some efficiency improvements, some extra money.
Content_zh: 縮一點範圍、提升一點效率、再多弄一點錢。

Content: We've been working on simplifying some stuff.
Content_zh: 我們一直在簡化一些東西。

Content: J.P.'s come on and we've been trying to look at things in the spaceship we can simplify.
Content_zh: J.P. 加進來之後，我們一直在看太空船裡有哪些東西可以簡化。

Content: We cut some room, cut the teleporter rooms, cut some simple stuff.
Content_zh: 我們砍了一些房間、砍掉傳送室，也砍了一些簡單的東西。

Content: But it's more like an ongoing thing, simplifying it as we go.
Content_zh: 但這比較像是持續進行的事，一邊做一邊簡化。

Content: You know, you want to make the game, most of the backers I think would be unanimous in saying they want a great game, right?
Content_zh: 你知道，我們想把遊戲做出來，而大多數贊助者應該都會一致說，他們想要一款很棒的遊戲，對吧？

Content: So they're trusting me to use the judgment of like how much to cut that still makes it a great game.
Content_zh: 所以他們是在相信我的判斷，相信我知道要砍到什麼程度，還能讓它是一款好遊戲。

Content: And they don't want us to cut more than that, so we just have to, we've been working on that problem.
Content_zh: 他們也不希望我們砍超過那個程度，所以我們一直在處理這個問題。

Content: Bailey: I'm not privy to what's actually going on in the scoping side right now.
Content_zh: Bailey：我現在不太清楚範圍調整那邊實際上的進展。

Content: Although I hear that they're, they're making some progress.
Content_zh: 不過我聽說他們有一些進展。

Content: On the funding side I was able to bring in a few deals that don't require any kind of creative control.
Content_zh: 資金方面，我談成了幾個不需要交出創意控制權的合作。

Content: So like Brutal Legend PC, which to me is potentially, you know, better on PC than it would be on console, because it's an RTS.
Content_zh: 像是《Brütal Legend》PC 版，對我來說，它在 PC 上可能比主機上更好，因為它是 RTS。

Content: It has RTS elements.
Content_zh: 它有 RTS 元素。

Content: It's never actually been released and it's got, you know, huge amount of music already licensed in it
Content_zh: 它其實從來沒發行過，而且裡面已經授權了大量音樂。

Content: So we moved that up and you know, we're looking to release that pretty soon.
Content_zh: 所以我們把那個往前排，打算很快推出。

Content: And all or, no, some or all of those profits will probably go back to Red's.
Content_zh: 那些利潤全部，呃，不，部分或全部大概會回到 Red’s。

Content: Schafer: I mean...for one of our other games, he found a licensing deal in Korea
Content_zh: Schafer：我是說……我們另一款遊戲，他在韓國找到一個授權合作。

Content: So there's extra money and revenue that can come in through that.
Content_zh: 所以那邊也能帶來額外的錢和收入。

Content: And that's the kind of stuff I would never have, I can't whip up a Korean licensing deal on my own, I'd have no idea.
Content_zh: 這種事我根本做不來，我沒辦法自己變出一個韓國授權案，完全不知道該怎麼辦。

Content: How do you, who do you even call? Do you know any-?
Content_zh: 你要怎麼……你到底該打給誰？你認識什麼……？

Content: We got such a response to Majus's print from the last episode that we can now, now selling the print.
Content_zh: 上一集 Majus 的版畫反應很好，所以我們現在可以賣那張版畫。

Content: Each one is a new line of dialogue that I can put in the game, so think about it that way.
Content_zh: 每賣一張，就等於我能在遊戲裡多放一句新對白，你們就這樣想。

Content: Bailey: I don't know how much money is actually going to be enough.
Content_zh: Bailey：我不知道到底多少錢才真的夠。

Content: So for me, like my mandate is more.
Content_zh: 所以對我來說，我的任務就是弄更多錢。

Content: Because I want Tim to be able to completely check out and when he feels the game's done, then it's done.
Content_zh: 因為我希望 Tim 可以完全照自己的判斷來，當他覺得遊戲完成了，那才算完成。

Content: Stamos: New addition to my office.
Content_zh: Stamos：我辦公室的新東西。

Content: It's pretty exciting.
Content_zh: 滿令人興奮的。

Content: There's nothing much on it yet.
Content_zh: 上面還沒什麼東西。

Content: Oh, except there's a red milestone right there.
Content_zh: 喔，除了那邊有一個紅色里程碑。

Content: I want to see how that goes.
Content_zh: 我想看看那會怎麼發展。

Content: We're working on all kinds of things throughout the whole studio.
Content_zh: 整個工作室都在做各式各樣的事。

Content: But when it comes to Red's we're I come in as more of a "how can we control costs?"
Content_zh: 但說到 Red’s，我比較像是從「我們要怎麼控制成本？」這個角度加入。

Content: Hansen: This still is adding up to six and a half months of tech work so [laughing] we gotta find out.
Content_zh: Hansen：這樣加起來還是有六個半月的技術工作，所以[笑聲]我們得弄清楚。

Content: Franzke: Oh that's just even split across all the ...
Content_zh: Franzke：喔，那只是平均分到所有……

Content: Hanson: Well it was eight and a half months before Franzke: But that actually makes sense to me, that this is kind of like a relief [laughs].
Content_zh: Hanson：之前是八個半月。Franzke：但這其實滿合理的，這樣反而有點鬆一口氣[笑聲]。

Content: Stamos: More, more needs to be done to really understand what's left to do and how long that will take.
Content_zh: Stamos：還要再做更多，才能真正了解剩下什麼、需要多久。

Content: Really like kind of refactoring all of our assumptions about that schedule.
Content_zh: 基本上就是重新整理我們對那份時程的所有假設。

Content: Really I think getting some project management attention on it.
Content_zh: 我覺得真的需要讓專案管理更關注這件事。

Content: Matt Hansen coming in and working on that uber-schedule so we can really plan out what's happening a little bit more down the road.
Content_zh: Matt Hansen 會加入，處理那份超級總時程，這樣我們才能更完整地規劃接下來會發生什麼。

Content: Hansen: Obviously Greg's already the producer on the project.
Content_zh: Hansen：Greg 顯然已經是這個專案的製作人。

Content: But you know, it's super-important for the studio, really important for us.
Content_zh: 但你知道，這對工作室超級重要，對我們也真的很重要。

Content: And you know, it's one of our completely self-funded projects so it's one of the things where it did make sense
Content_zh: 而且這是我們完全自籌資金的專案之一，所以在這種情況下，

Content: to bring on additional production help to make sure that we're, we're running as effectively and efficiently as possible.
Content_zh: 再增加製作方面的支援是合理的，確保我們盡可能有效率地運作。

Content: I have some expertise from working in adventure games in the past so I think that's something they wanted to bring onto the team
Content_zh: 我以前做過冒險遊戲，有一些相關經驗，我想這也是他們想讓我加入團隊的原因。

Content: to help make it go smoothly as possible.
Content_zh: 希望能讓事情盡可能順利。

Content: I'm actually pulling a lot from my experiences on those games to understand how to schedule the game, scope it properly and stuff like that.
Content_zh: 我其實大量借用以前做那些遊戲的經驗，來思考怎麼排時程、怎麼正確控管範圍等等。

Content: Hansen: I still have this remote deployment harness as something I'm going to have to talk to Tim about.
Content_zh: Hansen：我還有這個遠端部署框架要跟 Tim 談。

Content: I really want to push forward on that.
Content_zh: 我真的很想把那件事往前推。

Content: I think it's going to be really helpful for debugging the game later on.
Content_zh: 我覺得那對之後除錯會很有幫助。

Content: Franzke: I agree.
Content_zh: Franzke：我同意。

Content: Otherwise we're just going to shoot ourselves in the foot. Hansen: Yeah.
Content_zh: 不然我們就是在搬石頭砸自己的腳。Hansen：對。

Content: Rice: And yeah, it's been helpful.
Content_zh: Rice：對，這很有幫助。

Content: I think it's just like , it's nice to be able to kind of hand it off to him and kind of get ahead of things a little bit.
Content_zh: 我覺得能把一些事情交給他，讓我們稍微提前處理，真的很不錯。

Content: It's going to be really helpful.
Content_zh: 這會很有幫助。

Content: Hansen: Developing an engine and a game at the same time, it's always been a challenge for a lot of different games.
Content_zh: Hansen：同時開發引擎和遊戲，對很多遊戲來說一直都是挑戰。

Content: You'll hear that as a common story for a lot of games where they're, you know, it's like, Limbo's a good example,
Content_zh: 很多遊戲都有類似的故事，像《Limbo》就是很好的例子。

Content: where they were trying to develop the engine for Limbo, they're also trying to create the game at the same time.
Content_zh: 他們一邊為《Limbo》開發引擎，一邊也在做遊戲本身。

Content: It's huge, hugely challenging.
Content_zh: 那非常、非常有挑戰性。

Content: Hansen: All right. That's it.
Content_zh: Hansen：好，就這樣。

Content: Thank you very much.
Content_zh: 非常謝謝。

Content: I'll start laying this out and then let's talk about maybe later.
Content_zh: 我會先把這個整理出來，晚點我們再聊。

Content: Franzke: Yep. Hansen: Later next week. Thanks a lot.
Content_zh: Franzke：好。Hansen：下週晚一點。謝啦。

Content: Stamos: So that's been huge. I think that having additional gameplay programmers added to the team,
Content_zh: Stamos：所以這幫助很大。我覺得團隊增加了幾位 gameplay 程式設計師，

Content: but who have design experience, that that's probably helping.
Content_zh: 而且他們有設計經驗，應該很有幫助。

Content: Rice: Yeah, we just added a handful of people to the team, kind of staffing up a bit.
Content_zh: Rice：對，我們剛替團隊加了幾個人，算是補了一些人手。

Content: And it was people that were on The Cave and that just shipped a few weeks ago.
Content_zh: 這些人之前在做《The Cave》，那款幾週前才推出。

Content: [Applause] Gilbert: Well, we shipped The Cave.
Content_zh: [掌聲] Gilbert：嗯，我們推出了《The Cave》。

Content: [Cheering] Gilbert: On 742 platforms, so that's good news.
Content_zh: [歡呼聲] Gilbert：在 742 個平台上，所以這是好消息。

Content: Anyway, I just, you know, a toast to everybody and they just did an amazing job on The Cave.
Content_zh: 總之，我想敬大家一杯，大家在《The Cave》上真的做得很棒。

Content: Group: Cheers!
Content_zh: Group：乾杯！

Content: And now what you have all really been waiting for, cake.
Content_zh: 接下來才是大家真正期待的，蛋糕。

Content: Group: [laughs] Schafer: Scoop it up with his hands
Content_zh: Group：[笑聲] Schafer：用手把它挖起來。

Content: Put it in everyone's mouth Line up.
Content_zh: 塞進每個人嘴裡，排隊。

Content: Rice: And so they just are wrapping up their final tasks and bug fixes for that And as they become available, we're just trying to figure out
Content_zh: Rice：所以他們正在收尾最後的工作和 bug 修正。等他們有空，我們就要想清楚

Content: what makes the most sense for him to be working on and right now that is Reds.
Content_zh: 讓他們做什麼最合理，而目前答案就是 Reds。

Content: So we've got someone from every discipline added to the team and kind of helping to lighten people's loads and add some extra manpower to the game.
Content_zh: 所以我們讓每個職能都有人加入團隊，幫大家分擔工作，也替遊戲增加一些人力。

Content: Schafer: Everyone's here, OK. Awesome.
Content_zh: Schafer：大家都到了，好，太棒了。

Content: Dailies. Film crew's back.
Content_zh: 每日站會。攝影組回來了。

Content: Not that you notice.
Content_zh: 雖然你們不一定會注意到。

Content: I'm sure you guys are all, for those of you who have not ever been filmed before, if you ignore them, really,
Content_zh: 我相信你們都……如果有人以前沒被拍過，其實只要無視他們，真的。

Content: They'll just, they'll catch you picking your nose.
Content_zh: 他們只會拍到你挖鼻孔。

Content: Rice: We're too big now. I don't know how they're going to do this.
Content_zh: Rice：我們現在人太多了。我不知道他們要怎麼拍。

Content: Schafer: I know, look how huge we are.
Content_zh: Schafer：我知道，看我們多龐大。

Content: Let's get this over with. OK.
Content_zh: 快把這個弄完吧。好。

Content: Yesterday I was going to write those last three things for the Cloud Colony, but I did a whole bunch of other project review stuff.
Content_zh: 昨天我本來要寫雲端殖民地最後那三件事，但我做了一大堆其他專案檢視。

Content: LeBreton: It was pretty easy to pick up on, you know.
Content_zh: LeBreton：其實滿容易上手的。

Content: Anna was teaching me a whole bunch about the systems that she wrote on the game play,
Content_zh: Anna 教了我很多她寫的 gameplay 系統。

Content: which is mainly what I've been working with.
Content_zh: 那主要就是我一直在處理的東西。

Content: Just figuring out how, how dialogue, I've been, a lot of my work has been wiring up the dialogue trees, you know.
Content_zh: 我一直在弄清楚對話怎麼運作，我很多工作都是把對話樹接起來。

Content: We got the writing from Tim and then we got scratch VO, so I wired that up so you can pick, "tell me more"
Content_zh: 我們拿到 Tim 寫的稿，還有暫用 VO，所以我把它接起來，讓你可以選「多告訴我一點」

Content: or "what about this" or "see you later." Thomas: I guess you're better at spotting fakes than the other birds.
Content_zh: 或「那這個呢」或「回頭見」。Thomas：看來你比其他鳥更會認出假貨。

Content: Hey, can you take me here to my home?
Content_zh: 嘿，你可以帶我回我家嗎？

Content: You can? Great!
Content_zh: 可以？太好了！

Content: Ever seen this place? Great.
Content_zh: 看過這個地方嗎？太好了。

Content: Can you take me there right now?
Content_zh: 你現在可以帶我去嗎？

Content: LeBreton: I enjoy all phases of a project.
Content_zh: LeBreton：我喜歡專案的每個階段。

Content: You know, if it's a good project then it's fun to be in on the early inception where you're getting to like, you know,
Content_zh: 如果是好專案，早期構思階段會很好玩，因為你可以，嗯，

Content: help really make big important creative decisions.
Content_zh: 幫忙做出很重大、很重要的創意決策。

Content: It's fun to be in the middle where you're producing stuff, you know, you're building it
Content_zh: 中期也很好玩，因為你在產出東西、把它做出來。

Content: and eventually hit that critical mass where it's like, yes, we have a game now; we know what we're building.
Content_zh: 最後到達臨界點，你會覺得，對，我們現在有一款遊戲了，我們知道自己在做什麼。

Content: And then late in the game, if you're not, you know, if you're not stressing out too much about like, you know,
Content_zh: 到了後期，如果你沒有太焦慮，沒有一直被那些，嗯，

Content: fires that you have to put out, it's really fun to like see the last few steps come together
Content_zh: 需要滅火的事情壓垮，看著最後幾步慢慢成形真的很有趣。

Content: and really feel like some, like you're putting polish on something.
Content_zh: 你會真的覺得自己在替某個東西打磨收尾。

Content: So all of those are cool.
Content_zh: 所以那些階段都很酷。

Content: But yeah, I think we're very much in the middle.
Content_zh: 不過對，我覺得我們現在很明顯在中期。

Content: We're kind of like in the early middle in this.
Content_zh: 比較像是中期偏早的階段。

Content: So yeah, no.
Content_zh: 所以，對，也不是。

Content: And I've worked, you know, I've, yeah, I've led stuff before but also just been in the trenches guy.
Content_zh: 我做過很多事，也帶過東西，但也當過第一線埋頭做的人。

Content: So you know, depends, again, if it's a good project, then I'll enjoy, and I can contribute something substantial to it.
Content_zh: 所以還是要看情況。如果是好專案，我就會享受，也能做出實質貢獻。

Content: That's all I really care about.
Content_zh: 我在乎的其實就這樣。

Content: Garner: It was on budget, on time; everything was great.
Content_zh: Garner：預算內、準時，一切都很好。

Content: You're going to, you're going to go home early, no overtime.
Content_zh: 你會早點回家，不用加班。

Content: Then I saw Episode 7 and I was like, aha.
Content_zh: 然後我看了第七集，就想，啊哈。

Content: Just the normal stuff.
Content_zh: 就是平常那些事。

Content: They were just like, would you be interested in DFA, Tim wants you on that project so bad.
Content_zh: 他們就說，你會不會對 DFA 有興趣，Tim 超想要你加入那個專案。

Content: He's always talking about Dave being in on the project.
Content_zh: 他一直在說希望 Dave 加入這個專案。

Content: Schafer: We got Dave Garner, famous fan of Ico and Shadow of the Colossus.
Content_zh: Schafer：我們找來了 Dave Garner，知名的《Ico》和《Shadow of the Colossus》粉絲。

Content: Garner: That shit is so awesome.
Content_zh: Garner：那些東西超棒的。

Content: Schafer: And adventure games.
Content_zh: Schafer：還有冒險遊戲。

Content: Yes, Garner's here because adventure games are not dead.
Content_zh: 沒錯，Garner 會在這裡，是因為冒險遊戲還沒死。

Content: Garner: Adventure games are not dead.
Content_zh: Garner：冒險遊戲還沒死。

Content: Garner: When I came on, I initially, like the characters have so much style in them.
Content_zh: Garner：我加入時，一開始覺得這些角色非常有風格。

Content: The first thing that I thought about was like, I'm just going to like focus on animating real subtle stuff
Content_zh: 我第一個想到的是，我要專注在那些很細微的動畫。

Content: and like focus on the face and like focus on the eyes.
Content_zh: 像是臉部，還有眼睛。

Content: That stuff's important in like films and stuff but we don't usually do that stuff in games.
Content_zh: 那些東西在電影裡很重要，但我們在遊戲裡通常不太做。

Content: But I feel like that's one of the things that we want to try to do with this one.
Content_zh: 但我覺得這次我們想試著做到這件事。

Content: Garner: This is when we were experimenting with like, you know, what kind of an attitude should he have?
Content_zh: Garner：這是我們在實驗他應該有什麼樣態度的時候。

Content: Like sort of blank look on his face, that's like kind of bored because he's on the ship and getting sick of this stuff.
Content_zh: 像是臉上有點空洞的表情，因為他在船上，對這些事有點厭煩。

Content: And he's got like, you know, his idles would be like, he's just like, overall he's not too stoked on all the stuff that's going on, on the ship.
Content_zh: 他的待機動作大概就是，整體來說，他對船上發生的一切沒那麼興奮。

Content: But then when you start playing with him and explore, like we were saying it'll start getting really cool
Content_zh: 可是當你開始操控他、探索的時候，就像我們說的，事情會開始變得很酷。

Content: when he's just going to be like, oh my God!
Content_zh: 他會變成那種，天啊！

Content: This is awesome!
Content_zh: 這太棒了！

Content: A lot of people aren't really going to notice that, but like, hopeful some person is like, oh yeah, that's really cool.
Content_zh: 很多人可能不會注意到，但希望有人會覺得，喔對，這真的很酷。

Content: Like that boy, like I've seen him change throughout the game, which is not something not I've really done with a character in a game before.
Content_zh: 像那個男孩，我看著他在整個遊戲裡改變，這是我以前沒怎麼在遊戲角色上做過的事。

Content: Is like have them go from, you know, one character type and, like actually end the hero's journey having gone through it,
Content_zh: 就是讓他從一種角色狀態出發，真的經歷完英雄旅程後收尾。

Content: make, you know, an emotional journey as well.
Content_zh: 也讓他有一段情感上的旅程。

Content: Which is something you see in movies, just not so much in games.
Content_zh: 這在電影裡常看到，但遊戲裡比較少。

Content: Schafer: Who else got added?
Content_zh: Schafer：還加了誰？

Content: We got Lydia.
Content_zh: 我們有 Lydia。

Content: We've had Lydia for a little while.
Content_zh: Lydia 已經加入一陣子了。

Content: Lydia's a great effects artist, technical artist.
Content_zh: Lydia 是很棒的特效美術、技術美術。

Content: Does a little programming on the side.
Content_zh: 也會順手寫一點程式。

Content: Small teams, everyone has to do a bunch of stuff.
Content_zh: 小團隊嘛，每個人都得做很多事。

Content: Choy: Generally I just go through and just try to add things wherever I can, because like with this Cloud Colony.
Content_zh: Choy：通常我就是到處看，能加東西的地方就盡量加，像這個雲端殖民地。

Content: The more, the more there's like little support ambient effects, the more alive it feels.
Content_zh: 越多小的輔助環境特效，它就越有生命力。

Content: Schafer: I think this ground looks so pretty now.
Content_zh: Schafer：我覺得這塊地現在看起來真漂亮。

Content: And the little dust, and the poofs and everything.
Content_zh: 還有那些小灰塵、噗噗的效果，全部都很棒。

Content: And look at that thing up there!
Content_zh: 看上面那個東西！

Content: What's happening up there?
Content_zh: 上面發生什麼事？

Content: Is that 3D?
Content_zh: 那是 3D 嗎？

Content: Petty: Well, it's an old PlaysStation One trick.
Content_zh: Petty：嗯，那是老 PlayStation One 的技巧。

Content: We just took the texture and put it on crisscross polygons.
Content_zh: 我們只是拿貼圖放到交叉的多邊形上。

Content: And just spun it around in 3D.
Content_zh: 然後讓它在 3D 裡轉。

Content: Schafer: Oh now I hate it.
Content_zh: Schafer：喔，那我現在討厭它了。

Content: Now that I know.
Content_zh: 既然我知道了。

Content: Cheating, now I hate it.
Content_zh: 作弊，我現在討厭它了。

Content: It looks amazing.
Content_zh: 看起來超棒。

Content: Melian: So here's like a new thing we're trying out.
Content_zh: Melian：這是我們正在嘗試的新東西。

Content: It's actually using a 3D, a 3D element.
Content_zh: 它其實用了 3D，一個 3D 元素。

Content: And you'll see it again.
Content_zh: 之後你還會再看到。

Content: So we're experimenting with that now.
Content_zh: 所以我們現在正在試這個。

Content: Melian: I think so. I think that's the idea.
Content_zh: Melian：我想是。應該就是這個想法。

Content: If we can get these 3D elements working and kind of blending in there without popping out too much, I think that's the idea.
Content_zh: 如果我們能讓這些 3D 元素運作起來，又能融進畫面、不會太突兀，我想這就是方向。

Content: And we'll go in and try to do that more and more.
Content_zh: 我們會繼續進去，越做越多。

Content: Schafer: Whoa color!
Content_zh: Schafer：哇，顏色！

Content: Oh there's a mom!
Content_zh: 喔，有媽媽！

Content: Hey, hi, mom.
Content_zh: 嘿，嗨，媽。

Content: She's got hair that comes off of the crystal ball.
Content_zh: 她的頭髮是從水晶球上長出來的。

Content: Peck: I guess last week was like really my first time, my first week where like I really got to spend like a lot of my time on Red's.
Content_zh: Peck：我想上週算是我第一次，第一週真的能把很多時間花在 Red’s 上。

Content: Really fun.
Content_zh: 真的很好玩。

Content: Basically Lee's been working really hard getting the environment white boxed so you can actually just walk around.
Content_zh: 基本上 Lee 一直很努力把環境做成白模，讓你真的可以到處走。

Content: And I'm just following behind him wiring up the basic puzzle structure as it comes in.
Content_zh: 我就跟在他後面，把陸續進來的基本謎題結構接起來。

Content: Yeah, this is the first piece of color art we've got on this.
Content_zh: 對，這是我們這部分第一張彩色美術。

Content: Like a rough color.
Content_zh: 算是粗略上色。

Content: But still amazing.
Content_zh: 但還是很棒。

Content: It really reminds me of like, you have a giant art book, like hard cover picture book in front of you
Content_zh: 它真的讓我想到，好像你面前有一本巨大的精裝圖畫書。

Content: and just like turning the page and being really immersed in the art.
Content_zh: 就像翻頁，然後完全沉浸在美術裡。

Content: Like you know, with the old pixel-based adventure games, they didn't have like this camera flowing over things
Content_zh: 以前那些像素風冒險遊戲沒有這種鏡頭流動的感覺。

Content: and zooming in and panning out as much.
Content_zh: 也沒有這麼多拉近、平移。

Content: And this really feels like I'm being like guided through this, this really beautiful experience.
Content_zh: 而這真的像是有人帶著我走過一段很美的體驗。

Content: So that's what's really captured my imagination so far, is like this story is just being presented in such a beautiful way.
Content_zh: 所以目前最吸引我的，就是這個故事被用這麼美的方式呈現。

Content: I'm really excited to see like where, how far we can push that.
Content_zh: 我很期待看看我們能把它推到什麼程度。

Content: Peck: Let me just load up a certain level accepting.
Content_zh: Peck：我先載入某個關卡。

Content: Yeah, I'll open up the galley. Yeah.
Content_zh: 對，我來開 galley。對。

Content: Yeah, the bedroom and the galley have the rough color.
Content_zh: 對，臥室和 galley 都有粗略上色了。

Content: It's amazing. It's like super cool.
Content_zh: 很驚人，超酷的。

Content: Look how rough this is, but there's still this like epic scale.
Content_zh: 你看它還這麼粗，但已經有這種史詩感的尺度。

Content: You know, you're supposed to be floating out in space in a space suit, but everything but man.
Content_zh: 你應該是穿著太空衣漂在太空裡，但天啊，其他東西都……

Content: This really blew me away when I saw that. So yeah.
Content_zh: 我看到的時候真的被震撼到。所以對。

Content: I'm excited to talk to Bagel when he comes in and be like, awesome job, man.
Content_zh: 我很期待 Bagel 進來時跟他說，老兄，做得太棒了。

Content: Stapley: So yeah, I guess lightings down here and just try to tie it a little in with that one.
Content_zh: Stapley：所以對，我想這下面的打光要稍微跟那個連在一起。

Content: I feel like these are, I mean they're not totally different.
Content_zh: 我覺得這些……我是說，它們沒有完全不同。

Content: This color, I kinda like this being more white.
Content_zh: 這個顏色，我有點喜歡它更偏白。

Content: I feel like this is a little too orange.
Content_zh: 我覺得這有點太橘了。

Content: I mean, I guess it's just the orange light, but still, yeah.
Content_zh: 我是說，也許只是橘色的光，但還是，對。

Content: Maybe a little bit more yellow.
Content_zh: 也許再黃一點。

Content: Hey, Tim. I'll get him later. I say go for it.
Content_zh: 嘿，Tim。我晚點再找他。我覺得可以做。

Content: Brand: It's a really good learning experience.
Content_zh: Brand：這真的是很好的學習經驗。

Content: I really like Bagel's style.
Content_zh: 我真的很喜歡 Bagel 的風格。

Content: Yeah, he drew like a really awesome style guide that might pretty up the different areas of the ship.
Content_zh: 對，他畫了一份很棒的風格指南，可以讓船上不同區域變漂亮。

Content: You know, the vibe each one should give off and like the color palettes.
Content_zh: 像是每個區域該有的氛圍，還有色彩配置。

Content: And a lot of the references he was using to derive that look.
Content_zh: 還有很多他用來推導那種外觀的參考資料。

Content: So like the player living area, utility area and then there's an off-limits area for later in the game.
Content_zh: 像玩家生活區、機能區，還有遊戲後期的禁區。

Content: He took that and then did the, some even more like specific breakdowns with just a bunch of inspirational windows
Content_zh: 他拿那些再做更具體的拆解，整理出一堆靈感參考圖。

Content: that, with those shapes and colors that he likes.
Content_zh: 裡面有他喜歡的形狀和顏色。

Content: Stapley: I always go to orange.
Content_zh: Stapley：我總是會用橘色。

Content: Orange and brown and red is like shit that I always fucking do.
Content_zh: 橘色、棕色、紅色，就是我他媽一直會用的東西。

Content: I mean, I feel like, I mean it's a little crutch that I have.
Content_zh: 我是說，我覺得那有點像我的拐杖。

Content: Not a crutch but it's like my go-to color.
Content_zh: 不是拐杖啦，但就是我最常用的顏色。

Content: All the time.
Content_zh: 一直都是。

Content: But yeah man.
Content_zh: 不過對啦，老兄。

Content: You can nail it.
Content_zh: 你一定可以搞定。

Content: Stapley: Your colors are awesome.
Content_zh: Stapley：你的顏色很棒。

Content: Rice: And I'm excited to see the space stuff starting to come together, too, because Tim just delivered the dialogue for the boy's,
Content_zh: Rice：我也很期待看到太空那邊開始成形，因為 Tim 剛交了男孩那邊的對話。

Content: like first segment of the game and it's amazing.
Content_zh: 也就是遊戲第一段，真的很棒。

Content: It's hilarious.
Content_zh: 超好笑。

Content: And you can so easily see how fun it's going to be to play through.
Content_zh: 而且你很容易看出來，玩起來會有多有趣。

Content: Rice: Yeah, but like reading through it, I don't see any holes.
Content_zh: Rice：對，但讀過之後，我看不出有什麼漏洞。

Content: Like I feel like, Schafer: It's fucking perfect.
Content_zh: Rice：我覺得……Schafer：它他媽完美。

Content: Rice: I feel like the game play programmer could script that all up now without needing any questions, which is awesome.
Content_zh: Rice：我覺得 gameplay 程式設計師現在可以不用問任何問題，就把它全部 script 起來，這很棒。

Content: Schafer: So no questions will be allowed.
Content_zh: Schafer：所以不准問問題。

Content: Schafer: I think spaceship boy's stuff is easier, like the basic setup of it leads it to, it's funny because it's just, there's more humor in it.
Content_zh: Schafer：我覺得太空船男孩那邊比較容易，基本設定本身就比較好笑，因為裡面有更多幽默。

Content: And you would think you could just make everything funny equally but like the boy stuff's just naturally funnier
Content_zh: 你會以為所有東西都能做得一樣好笑，但男孩那邊天生就比較好笑。

Content: because he's all alone with his mom, the tension between him and his mom
Content_zh: 因為他跟媽媽單獨在一起，他和媽媽之間的張力，

Content: and all the little robots and all the silly scenes he finds himself in.
Content_zh: 還有那些小機器人，以及他身處的各種蠢場面。

Content: I have to figure out something for the girl's world that makes it that easy to put, to put jokes in there.
Content_zh: 我得替女孩的世界想出某種東西，讓笑點也能那麼容易放進去。

Content: Here's a picture of the climax of the game.
Content_zh: 這是遊戲高潮的圖片。

Content: Do you want to see my pictures?
Content_zh: 你想看我的圖片嗎？

Content: Total spoiler right there.
Content_zh: 這裡完全是劇透。

Content: Can you see that?
Content_zh: 你看得到嗎？

Content: Stamos: Yeah, I think that the change that I've seen the most is Tim, you know, absolutely.
Content_zh: Stamos：對，我覺得我看到最大的改變絕對是 Tim。

Content: Because he, he seems really motivated to kind of get things going.
Content_zh: 因為他看起來真的很有動力，想把事情推動起來。

Content: And also he's, he's getting really close to the point of the entire game's designed out.
Content_zh: 而且他也快要把整個遊戲設計完整了。

Content: And I think that will be a big, pretty big milestone.
Content_zh: 我覺得那會是個很大的里程碑。

Content: [Juicer whirs on] [Whooshing]
Content_zh: [榨汁機啟動聲] [呼呼聲]

Content: [Tim whistling] [Whooshing]
Content_zh: [Tim 吹口哨] [呼呼聲]

Content: Schafer: Lemon.
Content_zh: Schafer：檸檬。

Content: [Wet grinding] Schafer: The juice fast is all part of, you know,
Content_zh: [濕磨聲] Schafer：果汁斷食算是，嗯，

Content: when New Year's comes around you make a bunch of resolutions.
Content_zh: 新年到了你會立一堆新年新希望。

Content: And it's, my resolution was not to die before my daughter got in college.
Content_zh: 而我的新希望就是不要在女兒上大學前死掉。

Content: And also watching this documentary and seeing my gut enter the room like five minutes before I did.
Content_zh: 還有看這部紀錄片時，看到我的肚子比我本人早五分鐘進房間。

Content: It was just like, ah, got to do something about that.
Content_zh: 我就覺得，啊，得處理一下了。

Content: So now I look all shrunken and my neck's all wiggly.
Content_zh: 所以現在我看起來整個縮水，脖子也鬆垮垮的。

Content: So you just can't win.
Content_zh: 所以你怎樣都贏不了。

Content: You just can't win and I got a gallstone probably, so.
Content_zh: 你怎樣都贏不了，而且我可能還有膽結石，所以……

Content: Just lose, lose scenario, documentaries.
Content_zh: 紀錄片就是雙輸局面。

Content: [Whooshing slows down] Schafer: I need a glass. Did anyone else want juice?
Content_zh: [呼呼聲變慢] Schafer：我需要杯子。還有人要果汁嗎？

Content: Schafer: Yeah. I used to get a lot more sleepy in the afternoons and I don't know if that's coffee or that's just being super fat.
Content_zh: Schafer：對。我以前下午會更想睡，我不知道那是咖啡的關係，還是因為我超胖。

Content: Schafer: Alright. Pack up some juice to take with me.
Content_zh: Schafer：好。裝一點果汁讓我帶走。

Content: Cheers.
Content_zh: 乾杯。

Content: How much weight did I lose while I was drinking that?
Content_zh: 我喝那杯的時候瘦了幾公斤？

Content: Did you notice?
Content_zh: 你有注意到嗎？

Content: You see like shrinkage? Like, blugh.
Content_zh: 有看到縮水嗎？像這樣，呃。

Content: Schafer: According to the Internet, I'm like 230 right now.
Content_zh: Schafer：根據網路資料，我現在大概 230 磅。

Content: Schafer: Like one site said my weight should be 160 pounds.
Content_zh: Schafer：有個網站說我的體重應該是 160 磅。

Content: And I started this at 260.
Content_zh: 而我開始時是 260 磅。

Content: So I'm down 30 pounds.
Content_zh: 所以我瘦了 30 磅。

Content: Daughter: What?
Content_zh: Daughter：什麼？

Content: Schafer: You get to come shave off my beard.
Content_zh: Schafer：你可以來幫我刮鬍子。

Content: Daughter: Papa, I don't know how to shave.
Content_zh: Daughter：爸爸，我不會刮鬍子。

Content: Schafer: Well, there's a time in every girl's life when she needs to learn how to shave.
Content_zh: Schafer：每個女孩人生中總有一刻要學會刮鬍子。

Content: Schafer: Here we go. Here we go.
Content_zh: Schafer：來了，來了。

Content: [Buzzing] I'm really not going to do it.
Content_zh: [嗡嗡聲] 我真的不想刮。

Content: I'm not going to do it. This is terrible.
Content_zh: 我不要刮。這太可怕了。

Content: This is a horrible mistake. Look at that.
Content_zh: 這是個可怕的錯誤。你看。

Content: Schafer: I used to just grow a beard every Christmas, like a Christmas beard, it'd start around Thanksgiving.
Content_zh: Schafer：我以前每年聖誕節都會留鬍子，像聖誕鬍，大概從感恩節開始。

Content: Ray got me started on that.
Content_zh: 是 Ray 讓我開始這樣做的。

Content: And then one year I grew the Christmas beard and I gained so much weight while I had it on that when I went to shave it off, I was like holy crap!
Content_zh: 有一年我留了聖誕鬍，留著留著胖了好多，等我要刮掉時，我整個想說，天啊！

Content: Where was all this hiding?
Content_zh: 這些肉都藏在哪裡？

Content: And then I was like, I'm going grow it back.
Content_zh: 然後我就想，我要把鬍子留回來。

Content: 'Cause you actually believe that if you grow a beard, you can totally hide the fact that you're fat.
Content_zh: 因為你真的會相信只要留鬍子，就能完全掩蓋自己很胖這件事。

Content: No one will figure it out.
Content_zh: 沒有人會發現。

Content: I didn't mean to look at the people with beards when I said that.
Content_zh: 我剛剛那樣說時，不是故意看有鬍子的人。

Content: And as, like when I get down to that weight again, I'll shave it off.
Content_zh: 然後我想，等我再瘦回那個體重，我就把它刮掉。

Content: And so I shaved it off.
Content_zh: 所以我就刮掉了。

Content: Schafer: I feel really dumb with this.
Content_zh: Schafer：我這樣覺得好蠢。

Content: Try to get respect in a meeting.
Content_zh: 試著在會議裡讓人尊重你。

Content: I think here's a new design, you guys.
Content_zh: 各位，我想這裡有個新設計。

Content: [Laughter offscreen] [Buzzing]
Content_zh: [畫外笑聲] [嗡嗡聲]

Content: This is now, this is my look from like the '90s.
Content_zh: 現在這是我 90 年代的造型。

Content: It was most of '90s.
Content_zh: 90 年代大部分時間都是這樣。

Content: I'm going to go all the way, you guys.
Content_zh: 各位，我要一次刮到底。

Content: Schafer: If you think about it, the car is just a metaphor for my body.
Content_zh: Schafer：仔細想想，這台車就是我身體的隱喻。

Content: They're both essentially really good looking, but they've been, you know, neglected a little bit over the years.
Content_zh: 兩者本質上都很好看，只是這些年都有點疏於照顧。

Content: It's got some scratches.
Content_zh: 它有一些刮痕。

Content: There's a big knife hole in the top of it, just like me.
Content_zh: 車頂有個大刀洞，跟我一樣。

Content: It's a little rusty.
Content_zh: 有點生鏽。

Content: It doesn't start in the morning.
Content_zh: 早上發不動。

Content: It's full of gas.
Content_zh: 裡面充滿瓦斯。

Content: Etcetera, etcetera.
Content_zh: 諸如此類。

Content: And my car is something that I've been like just looking at, I should really take care of that eventually.
Content_zh: 我的車就是我一直看著，心想總有一天真的該好好處理的東西。

Content: Kind of like, like, ah, I should really lose this weight eventually.
Content_zh: 有點像，啊，我總有一天真的該減肥。

Content: So I tweeted like anyone know a good mechanic?
Content_zh: 所以我發推說，有人認識好的技師嗎？

Content: And of course, John Romero and Brenda Romero, like we have this guy who's perfect for a MoPar, which is what my Barracuda is.
Content_zh: 當然，John Romero 和 Brenda Romero 就說，我們認識一個很適合修 MoPar 的人，而我的 Barracuda 就是 MoPar。

Content: And I went down there and John showed up.
Content_zh: 我就過去了，John 也出現了。

Content: And of course I'm all excited about getting my car fixed.
Content_zh: 我當然很興奮，因為車要修好了。

Content: And then John pulls up in this amazing like, pretty much a brand-new, like Road Runner, like '68, '69 Road Runner.
Content_zh: 結果 John 開著一台超驚人的，幾乎全新的 Road Runner，大概是 68、69 年的 Road Runner。

Content: And it's all shiny.
Content_zh: 整台閃閃發亮。

Content: The engine is cleaner than anything in my house.
Content_zh: 引擎比我家任何東西都乾淨。

Content: Like you could eat off it.
Content_zh: 乾淨到可以在上面吃飯。

Content: It's beautiful.
Content_zh: 超漂亮。

Content: And my car's sitting there all sad.
Content_zh: 而我的車就很悲傷地停在那裡。

Content: Schafer: My sad old car.
Content_zh: Schafer：我那台悲傷的老車。

Content: Schafer: Which brings out a whole bunch of different issues for me.
Content_zh: Schafer：這讓我冒出一大堆不同的問題。

Content: Like why aren't I rich like John Romero?
Content_zh: 像是為什麼我不像 John Romero 那麼有錢？

Content: Next year I'll get on that.
Content_zh: 明年我再來處理那個。

Content: This year I'm just going to get the car fixed.
Content_zh: 今年我先把車修好。

Content: And I started feeling the money flow out of my body as the guy was talking about all the work he wanted to do on it.
Content_zh: 那個人開始講他想對車做哪些工時，我就感覺錢從我身體裡流出去。

Content: Schafer: How expensive is this going to be? I just want to get it running.
Content_zh: Schafer：這會有多貴？我只是想讓它能跑。

Content: Schafer: So I need this game to be a hit to get out of the debt I'm going to accrue by getting that car running again.
Content_zh: Schafer：所以我需要這款遊戲大賣，才能還掉我為了讓那台車重新跑起來欠下的債。

Content: [Engine roars] Schafer: I think the situation with our cars
Content_zh: [引擎轟鳴] Schafer：我覺得我們車子的狀況

Content: really shows our levels of financial success in the industry.
Content_zh: 真的反映了我們在這個產業裡的財務成功程度。

Content: Schafer: And then John took me for the most terrifying drive of my life.
Content_zh: Schafer：然後 John 載我去兜了我人生最恐怖的一趟車。

Content: [Engine accelerates] Schafer: Jesus Christ!
Content_zh: [引擎加速] Schafer：我的天啊！

Content: [Romero laughs] Schafer: It was like, in the car, he hit the gas and it felt like,
Content_zh: [Romero 笑聲] Schafer：在車裡，他一踩油門，感覺就像，

Content: like a sledge hammer just punched in the back of my neck.
Content_zh: 好像有把大鐵鎚直接敲到我後頸。

Content: Just snapped back like Aaaagghh!
Content_zh: 整個往後甩，啊啊啊！

Content: [Engine roar; screaming] And then John's driving.
Content_zh: [引擎轟鳴；尖叫聲] 然後 John 在開車。

Content: I'm sure John is a really good driver, but I'm not 100 percent sure about that.
Content_zh: 我相信 John 是很好的駕駛，但我不是百分之百確定。

Content: [Engine roar] Schafer: I thought I was gonna die.
Content_zh: [引擎轟鳴] Schafer：我以為我死定了。

Content: Romero: Yeah! [Laughs] Thanks for the ride, John.
Content_zh: Romero：耶！[笑聲] 謝啦，John，感謝載我。

Content: [Booing; Jeers] Offscreen: Who are you?
Content_zh: [噓聲；嘲弄聲] 畫外音：你是誰？

Content: Offscreen: Looks like a baby!
Content_zh: 畫外音：看起來像嬰兒！

Content: Schafer: All right. I know I don't have the authority anymore.
Content_zh: Schafer：好啦。我知道我現在沒有威嚴了。

Content: Just look at me.
Content_zh: 看看我。

Content: You'll get used to it.
Content_zh: 你們會習慣的。

Content: Schafer: I shaved the beard, I'm on a diet.
Content_zh: Schafer：我刮了鬍子，也在節食。

Content: Fixed the car.
Content_zh: 車也修好了。

Content: I'm getting my writing done.
Content_zh: 我也在把劇本寫完。

Content: It's just a new, all new me.
Content_zh: 這就是全新的我。

Content: For a couple months.
Content_zh: 大概維持幾個月。

Content: Then i'll just go back.
Content_zh: 然後我就會變回去。

Content: Schafer: Red's. Let go to projects. Red's, We finished the sprint on Friday and we're now looking at the build
Content_zh: Schafer：Red’s。來看專案。Red’s，我們星期五完成了 sprint，現在正在看 build，

Content: and trying to see how we get ready to actually play-test and maybe a HOF-style play-test with you guys.
Content_zh: 想看看要怎麼準備實際 playtest，也許跟大家做一次 HOF 式的 playtest。

Content: Who would like to do that.
Content_zh: 誰想做這個？

Content: Maybe even, well, soon. Soon.
Content_zh: 也許甚至，嗯，很快。很快。

Content: Let's just say soon.
Content_zh: 就說很快吧。

Content: Rice: That stands for Hour of Fun.
Content_zh: Rice：那是 Hour of Fun 的縮寫。

Content: It's something that's been happening here at Double Fine for a long time.
Content_zh: 這是在 Double Fine 已經做很久的事。

Content: Schafer: All right. Everyone excited about the HOF?
Content_zh: Schafer：好。大家期待 HOF 嗎？

Content: First HOF.
Content_zh: 第一次 HOF。

Content: Who's new, raise your hand, if you've never been in a HOF?
Content_zh: 新來的人舉手，如果你從沒參加過 HOF？

Content: So you've all done HOFs before.
Content_zh: 所以你們都參加過 HOF。

Content: Let me tell you about the Hour of Fun.
Content_zh: 我來跟你們說 Hour of Fun 是什麼。

Content: Schafer: So way back on Psychonauts, we found a lot of people had not played the game, like some of the animators had never booted up and played it.
Content_zh: Schafer：很久以前做《Psychonauts》時，我們發現很多人沒玩過遊戲，像有些動畫師從沒開起來玩過。

Content: I was like, how are we going to understand our game unless everybody plays it?
Content_zh: 我就想，如果不是每個人都玩過，我們要怎麼理解自己的遊戲？

Content: So we had the mandatory Hour of Fun on a Friday.
Content_zh: 所以我們在星期五辦了強制參加的 Hour of Fun。

Content: Everybody had to stop working and play the game for an hour.
Content_zh: 所有人都要停下工作，玩遊戲一小時。

Content: And then we did this thing where we met afterwards and be like, ask everybody what they thought.
Content_zh: 然後我們之後開會，問每個人覺得怎麼樣。

Content: And it was really terrifying that people actually talked about it, but really good because we got a lot of good feedback.
Content_zh: 大家真的開始談這件事，這很可怕，但也很好，因為我們得到很多好回饋。

Content: And so we did it all through Brutal.
Content_zh: 所以我們做《Brütal Legend》時也一路這樣做。

Content: We'd play the game and then everybody would just get to say whatever they felt about it.
Content_zh: 我們會玩遊戲，然後每個人都能說出自己的感受。

Content: Schafer: When you're milling around, talking to people, try not to, we try to not to influence people too much when they're playing the game,
Content_zh: Schafer：你們到處走、跟人聊天時，盡量不要……我們盡量不要在別人玩遊戲時影響他們太多。

Content: because we'd really like to get, I think the most important feedback is getting people to talk about what they're trying to do,
Content_zh: 因為我覺得最重要的回饋，是讓玩家說出他們正在嘗試做什麼。

Content: because that's the stuff you miss in adventure games a lot, is like they're dragging this object that doesn't make any sense.
Content_zh: 因為冒險遊戲常會漏掉這種事，例如他們拖著一個完全沒道理的物件。

Content: And they're like oh, well, 'cause everyone knows the bananas are flammable; I'm just putting them in, like oh God.
Content_zh: 他們會說，喔，因為大家都知道香蕉易燃，所以我要把它放進去。你就會想，天啊。

Content: Like we never thought of that.
Content_zh: 我們根本沒想到那個。

Content: So catching what people are trying to do, getting them talking out loud is really good.
Content_zh: 所以抓到玩家想做什麼、讓他們大聲說出來，真的很有用。

Content: For the most part just watching, observing people and just, we don't, just try not to, there's a tendency you want to explain, like oh my God!
Content_zh: 大部分時間就只是看著、觀察他們。你會很想解釋，會想說，天啊！

Content: That's, that's going to be much better when you play.
Content_zh: 那個你之後玩的時候會好很多。

Content: Don't worry about that, that's going to be really great.
Content_zh: 別擔心，那會變得很棒。

Content: Just don't even bother with that.
Content_zh: 那個完全不用管。

Content: Just let them, let them hate the game, 'cause you know, the next HOF they'll be like, oh my God, you got so much more improvements in.
Content_zh: 就讓他們討厭遊戲吧，因為下次 HOF 他們就會說，天啊，你們改進好多。

Content: It was great. OK.
Content_zh: 那很好。好。

Content: This is going to be exciting. Whoo.
Content_zh: 這會很刺激。呼。

Content: Schafer: Everybody will be playing the game and then meeting afterwards to say, I don't like this game.
Content_zh: Schafer：大家都會玩遊戲，然後之後開會說，我不喜歡這款遊戲。

Content: Or I love this game.
Content_zh: 或是我愛這款遊戲。

Content: Then we'll also have an anonymous survey where people can say I just don't like Tim, whatever.
Content_zh: 我們也會有匿名問卷，讓大家可以說，我就是不喜歡 Tim 之類的。

Content: Rice: So we're going to be doing that on the Cloud Colony this afternoon.
Content_zh: Rice：所以我們今天下午會在雲端殖民地做這件事。

Content: We've been working to kind of polish that level up a bit and get a lot of the details in there
Content_zh: 我們一直在稍微打磨那個關卡，把很多細節放進去。

Content: that a first time player would need to be able to understand what's going on.
Content_zh: 讓第一次玩的玩家能理解發生什麼事。

Content: Franzke: So the main things that we need, just make it playable like, can you basically go through the entire Cloud Colony
Content_zh: Franzke：我們主要需要的是讓它可玩，也就是你基本上能走完整個雲端殖民地，

Content: without needing to load, reload the game and stuff like that.
Content_zh: 不用載入、重開遊戲之類的。

Content: Well, the major things that I am focused on is mostly crashes.
Content_zh: 我主要關注的事情大多是當機。

Content: Really, it's kind of anything that seriously goes wrong under the hood where stuff just falls over and dies.
Content_zh: 說真的，就是底層嚴重出錯、東西整個倒掉死掉的那種問題。

Content: And it's good we got test support last week a little bit, so finally testers playing the game.
Content_zh: 好在我們上週得到一些測試支援，終於有測試員在玩遊戲了。

Content: And looking at stuff and they find things which is good.
Content_zh: 他們會看各種東西，也會找到問題，這很好。

Content: And then we can go in and fix those hopefully.
Content_zh: 然後希望我們就能進去修掉那些問題。

Content: Rice: Yeah, we finally testers on the game.
Content_zh: Rice：對，我們終於有測試員在測這款遊戲。

Content: So they've been, you know, finding bugs and putting them in our bug database for us to fix.
Content_zh: 所以他們一直在找 bug，並把 bug 放進我們的 bug 資料庫，讓我們修。

Content: Lo: Before I started working as a tester, I would basically spend eight hours a day playing video games every day.
Content_zh: Lo：在我開始當測試員之前，我基本上每天會花八小時玩電玩。

Content: And now that it's my job, I come home and I'm like, I still look at a computer monitor
Content_zh: 現在這變成我的工作，我回到家還是看著電腦螢幕，

Content: but I like, I don't really feel like playing games that even, that I've been excited for.
Content_zh: 但就連我原本很期待的遊戲，我也不太想玩。

Content: In fact, I think it actually made me less excited for certain games.
Content_zh: 事實上，我覺得這讓我對某些遊戲更沒那麼期待。

Content: I'm only excited for like games that I really love, whereas before I was like, oh, I kind of like that game.
Content_zh: 現在我只會期待真正很愛的遊戲，以前則會覺得，喔，我好像滿喜歡那款。

Content: I might buy it.
Content_zh: 也許我會買。

Content: And I'll play it.
Content_zh: 然後我會玩。

Content: It actually made me more selective of the games that I would actually beat nowadays.
Content_zh: 這其實讓我現在更挑，只會把某些遊戲真的玩完。

Content: And it takes a lot for a game for me to actually complete it now.
Content_zh: 現在一款遊戲要讓我真的破關，需要很大的吸引力。

Content: So yeah.
Content_zh: 所以對。

Content: But before there was an issue where you pick up the ladder from that kid, and then you pick up the egg.
Content_zh: 但之前有個問題，你從那個小孩那裡拿起梯子，然後再拿起蛋。

Content: There would be, it would just crash the game for some reason.
Content_zh: 不知道為什麼，遊戲就會當掉。

Content: There's that.
Content_zh: 有這個問題。

Content: She just shoots in the top of ladder.
Content_zh: 她會直接衝到梯子頂端。

Content: She doesn't have any pupils for some reason.
Content_zh: 不知道為什麼她沒有瞳孔。

Content: Franzke: Very often it's like, like a big shaky house in the way like if you push it at the wrong place it would fall over.
Content_zh: Franzke：很多時候它就像一棟搖搖欲墜的大房子，你在錯的地方推一下就會倒。

Content: Just the way it is.
Content_zh: 它就是這樣。

Content: But yeah.
Content_zh: 不過對。

Content: The trick is to find, the trick is to find all those places that the user can directly influence.
Content_zh: 訣竅就是找出所有使用者能直接影響的地方。

Content: Directly or indirectly make sure that those are failsafe.
Content_zh: 不管直接或間接，都要確保那些地方有防呆。

Content: And any good.
Content_zh: 然後都沒問題。

Content: Schafer: OK. Thank you for coming to our very first HOF.
Content_zh: Schafer：好。謝謝大家來參加我們第一次 HOF。

Content: We have the build, ready to go.
Content_zh: 我們的 build 已經準備好了。

Content: Some of you might have installed it already.
Content_zh: 有些人可能已經安裝好了。

Content: Oliver sent out a mail, a copied it on my mail so you can just install it on your computer.
Content_zh: Oliver 寄了一封信，我也轉寄給大家，所以你們可以直接安裝到電腦上。

Content: And it should just magically run if you click on the "run" batch file.
Content_zh: 點一下「run」批次檔，它應該就會神奇地跑起來。

Content: It is a point-and-click graphic adventure.
Content_zh: 這是一款點擊式圖像冒險遊戲。

Content: Offscreen: What?
Content_zh: 畫外音：什麼？

Content: Offscreen Voice: They don't make those anymore.
Content_zh: 畫外音：現在已經沒人在做那種了。

Content: Offscreen Voice: Those are dead.
Content_zh: 畫外音：那種已經死了。

Content: Garner: Adventure games are not dead.
Content_zh: Garner：冒險遊戲還沒死。

Content: Schafer: Thank you.
Content_zh: Schafer：謝謝。

Content: Schafer: It has been almost one year since we launched our Kickstarter, by the way.
Content_zh: Schafer：順帶一提，距離我們啟動 Kickstarter 已經快一年了。

Content: And now we're having our first HOF.
Content_zh: 現在我們要辦第一次 HOF。

Content: And so we have done a lot of stuff to get this adventure game engine up and running and working
Content_zh: 我們做了很多事情，才讓這個冒險遊戲引擎啟動並運作起來。

Content: and we have a several room area of what's called the Cloud Colony to play.
Content_zh: 現在有一個叫雲端殖民地的區域，裡面有幾個房間可以玩。

Content: And we'd like to watch you play it.
Content_zh: 我們想看你們玩。

Content: Franzke: If it crashes like please come to any of the Red's programmers so we can help you, hopefully.
Content_zh: Franzke：如果它當掉，請找任何一位 Red’s 程式設計師，希望我們能幫上忙。

Content: Also if it crashes during the game, which hopefully it won't, please don't.
Content_zh: 另外如果遊戲中當掉，希望不會，請不要……

Content: If you press F1 and then go to "load" you can restore the previously, previous game state.
Content_zh: 如果你按 F1，再到「load」，就可以回復到前一個遊戲狀態。

Content: Schafer: OK, see you later.
Content_zh: Schafer：好，待會見。

Content: We'll call you back in here in about half an hour.
Content_zh: 大概半小時後我們會再叫大家回來。

Content: Schafer: What, you didn't find a bug, did you?
Content_zh: Schafer：什麼，你沒有找到 bug 吧？

Content: Kipnis: What? Schafer: You didn't find a bug, did you?
Content_zh: Kipnis：什麼？Schafer：你沒有找到 bug 吧？

Content: Stapley: To soften the blow of failure.
Content_zh: Stapley：為了減輕失敗的打擊。

Content: Yeah!
Content_zh: 耶！

Content: Offscreen Voice: Add something on to that, some choices, right.
Content_zh: 畫外音：在那裡加點東西，一些選項，對吧。

Content: I sent one to them.
Content_zh: 我有傳一個給他們。

Content: I think it was like, who's Jessie or something like that, right.
Content_zh: 我想好像是「Jessie 是誰」之類的，對吧。

Content: And then he said it, and I thought it would take me back to where I could ask something else, like something else about the towers.
Content_zh: 然後他說完後，我以為它會帶我回去，讓我可以問別的事，像關於塔的其他問題。

Content: I guess I'm going down, the talking tree or something like that.
Content_zh: 我想我是在走那棵會說話的樹那條線之類的。

Content: Raz: Man, I don't know, I don't know what, like what's the first thing to get, what to say, how do I kill this guy?
Content_zh: Raz：老兄，我不知道，不知道要先拿什麼、該說什麼、我要怎麼殺掉這傢伙？

Content: Rice: All the other one's good.
Content_zh: Rice：其他的都不錯。

Content: Have you seen the one here, if you, go ahead, nothing.
Content_zh: 你有看過這裡這個嗎？如果你……沒事，繼續。

Content: Schafer: We aren't talking about, Rice: We're talking about you.
Content_zh: Schafer：我們不是在說……Rice：我們在說你。

Content: Just keep going. They updated the one here.
Content_zh: 繼續。他們更新了這裡這個。

Content: If you aren't coming up that ladder, it's really nice.
Content_zh: 如果你不是從那個梯子爬上來，這裡真的不錯。

Content: Ryken: Adventure games are very quiet during HOFs, a lot of thinking going on.
Content_zh: Ryken：冒險遊戲在 HOF 時很安靜，大家都在思考。

Content: Petty: Oh, Drew crashed it.
Content_zh: Petty：喔，Drew 讓它當掉了。

Content: Schafer: What? Who crashed?
Content_zh: Schafer：什麼？誰當掉了？

Content: Kipnis: What was the last thing that you did, do you remember?
Content_zh: Kipnis：你最後做了什麼，還記得嗎？

Content: Inversin: I just went down the ladder and zoomed in on her and the curser appeared and then the arrow appeared and it just froze.
Content_zh: Inversin：我只是下梯子，鏡頭拉近到她身上，游標出現，然後箭頭出現，接著就凍住了。

Content: Franzke: Yeah, I'm not sure what's happening with that crash button.
Content_zh: Franzke：對，我不確定那個當機按鈕是怎麼回事。

Content: Chi: This is what happened when I went in here, so.
Content_zh: Chi：我進到這裡時就是發生這樣，所以……

Content: OK, this is where I crashed.
Content_zh: 好，我就是在這裡當掉的。

Content: Franzke: OK, thank you.
Content_zh: Franzke：好，謝謝。

Content: Franzke: That's the other thing, that Tony plays, through Visual Studio in debug.
Content_zh: Franzke：還有另一件事，Tony 是用 Visual Studio 的 debug 模式玩。

Content: And he could reproduce a new bug. So it's Windows only, XP only.
Content_zh: 他可以重現一個新 bug。所以只在 Windows，而且只在 XP。

Content: Dillon: That sounds like a fun bug to fix.
Content_zh: Dillon：聽起來是個很好修的 bug。

Content: Rice: That's the prettiest scene too.
Content_zh: Rice：那還是最漂亮的一幕。

Content: [Music] Schafer: Hello, hello.
Content_zh: [音樂] Schafer：哈囉，哈囉。

Content: [Murmuring] Schafer: Hello. Thank you for playing, thank you for playing Red's.
Content_zh: [低語聲] Schafer：大家好。謝謝你們來玩，謝謝你們玩 Red’s。

Content: The first people who have ever played Red's.
Content_zh: 你們是史上第一批玩 Red’s 的人。

Content: Patrick, you looked sad there for a second.
Content_zh: Patrick，你剛剛有一瞬間看起來很難過。

Content: Did you play?
Content_zh: 你有玩嗎？

Content: Hackett: The game was extremely charming.
Content_zh: Hackett：這款遊戲非常迷人。

Content: It's really good.
Content_zh: 真的很好。

Content: But...
Content_zh: 但是……

Content: [Laughter] Schafer: But...
Content_zh: [笑聲] Schafer：但是……

Content: Hackett: It's, there's just small things like I wanted to speed up some of the lines when they were repeating.
Content_zh: Hackett：就是有些小地方，像有些台詞重複時，我會想把它加速。

Content: I wanted an explanation of what was in my inventory.
Content_zh: 我想知道物品欄裡的東西是什麼。

Content: Franklin: The areas, someone said, I think it was a little unclear where the camera would pan and where it wouldn't.
Content_zh: Franklin：有人提到區域，我覺得有點不清楚鏡頭哪裡會平移、哪裡不會。

Content: Also as visually, maybe it wasn't clear if there was like, hey, there's more down there or there isn't.
Content_zh: 視覺上也可能不清楚下面到底還有沒有東西。

Content: That was a big one.
Content_zh: 這點很重要。

Content: Muir: I definitely had camera issues.
Content_zh: Muir：我確實遇到鏡頭問題。

Content: When it stops working, you're like, oh shit.
Content_zh: 它停止運作時，你會想，糟了。

Content: Like I can't go, you know,it's like I'm walking to the edge of the screen and like it's happening and then it gets really confusing.
Content_zh: 像是我走不到那裡，或我走到螢幕邊緣，事情發生了，然後就變得很混亂。

Content: So I mean, I guess it's just like a little buggy in some of those areas.
Content_zh: 所以我想那些地方就是有點 bug。

Content: But yeah, just no camera bugs, I would suggest.
Content_zh: 不過對，我的建議就是不要有鏡頭 bug。

Content: Yeah, that's my suggestion.
Content_zh: 對，這就是我的建議。

Content: Eliminate the camera bugs.
Content_zh: 消滅鏡頭 bug。

Content: Remo: Obviously the background art is incredibly gorgeous.
Content_zh: Remo：背景美術顯然漂亮得不得了。

Content: The whole game is really beautiful, but the specific style of the art means everything
Content_zh: 整款遊戲都很美，但這種特定美術風格會讓所有東西

Content: just looks like part of the background and I don't get that feeling that I often get in adventure games of like,
Content_zh: 看起來都像背景的一部分，我沒有冒險遊戲常會有的那種感覺，像是，

Content: oh, these are hot spots.
Content_zh: 喔，這些是熱點。

Content: I should be clicking these things.
Content_zh: 我應該點這些東西。

Content: And then when there were things that I thought I should be able to click on, like a big nest of eggs or something,
Content_zh: 然後當我看到我覺得應該能點的東西，像一大窩蛋之類的，

Content: instead my character would just try and path to like a plane behind it and go around, because it's not actually clickable.
Content_zh: 結果角色只是試著走到它後面的平面再繞過去，因為那其實不能點。

Content: So I felt like the game was discouraging me from trying to actually interact with things and find things,
Content_zh: 所以我覺得遊戲好像在阻止我嘗試跟東西互動、尋找東西。

Content: which is like to my memory, what adventure, playing adventure games, that feels like a big part of it.
Content_zh: 但在我的記憶裡，玩冒險遊戲時，那是很大一部分。

Content: And I felt like this game didn't want me to do that.
Content_zh: 而我覺得這款遊戲不希望我那樣做。

Content: Schafer: I'm a strong believer in being, having a thick skin about feedback.
Content_zh: Schafer：我非常相信面對回饋要臉皮厚一點。

Content: Like, 'cause when I've done writing, I've done writing in a writing group and you get together and you read your stuff and people give you feedback.
Content_zh: 因為我以前寫作時參加過寫作小組，大家聚在一起讀自己的東西，然後別人給你回饋。

Content: And you should be, you should say thank you when they say something bad about your script,
Content_zh: 當他們批評你的劇本時，你應該說謝謝。

Content: 'cause that's the thing that if you can turn it around, it now makes your script better or your writing better.
Content_zh: 因為如果你能把那點轉化掉，它就能讓你的劇本或文字變得更好。

Content: So any feedback we get, even if it's negative is like a gift, because that's just an opportunity to make the thing better.
Content_zh: 所以我們收到的任何回饋，就算是負面的，也像禮物一樣，因為那是讓東西變更好的機會。

Content: And it's better you hear it from your team than a game reviewer or someone in the public.
Content_zh: 而且從自己團隊聽到，總比從遊戲評論或大眾那裡聽到好。

Content: Boehle: As I just was exploring the dialogue, I kind of got a better sense of like, oh, I want to kill the creature but oh,
Content_zh: Boehle：我在探索對話時，逐漸更清楚，喔，我想殺掉那個生物，可是喔，

Content: I guess the next time the creature's going to be there in Shellmound.
Content_zh: 我想下次那個生物會出現在 Shellmound。

Content: and oh, I guess I need this sap to attract the creature in Shellmound.
Content_zh: 然後，喔，我想我需要這個樹液來吸引 Shellmound 的生物。

Content: and know I need to take the tram to get there.
Content_zh: 也知道我需要搭纜車過去。

Content: And so I liked how it was a very natural progression, just by talking to people without even having to solve puzzles,
Content_zh: 所以我喜歡它很自然地推進，只是跟人說話，甚至還不用解謎，

Content: just to figure out what I wanted to do as the character.
Content_zh: 就能弄清楚身為這個角色我想做什麼。

Content: Martz: And I agree that it has like a, kind of an open-ended quality, but I definitely would have appreciated some more like concrete goals.
Content_zh: Martz：我同意它有某種開放感，但我確實會希望有更多具體目標。

Content: Dawson: A little more back and forth between the characters.
Content_zh: Dawson：角色之間可以再多一點來回互動。

Content: Each kind of revealing a little bit of their world to each other.
Content_zh: 讓彼此稍微向對方揭露自己的世界。

Content: That might have filled you in a little.
Content_zh: 那可能會讓你更了解一點。

Content: Muir: I think I especially noticed it when I was talking to the Harmony dude.
Content_zh: Muir：我覺得我跟 Harm’ny 那傢伙說話時特別有感。

Content: And it was just like everything that he said was just like, oh my God, that's another thing that I don't know about.
Content_zh: 他說的每件事都讓我覺得，天啊，又是一個我不了解的東西。

Content: So like the whole world felt super-alien and just like, just super-weird.
Content_zh: 所以整個世界感覺超陌生，也超怪。

Content: Schafer: So you really have to get good at trying to like pry out of them what their experience is.
Content_zh: Schafer：所以你真的要很擅長把他們的體驗挖出來。

Content: Like if they think, well, this dialogue sequence is too long, but it's really that the music that was playing is looping too much
Content_zh: 例如他們覺得這段對話太長，但其實真正問題是播放的音樂循環太多次，

Content: while they're doing it or the dialogue is too long.
Content_zh: 或是真的對話太長。

Content: And you know, just, you got to, you got to take all that feedback and try to turn it into a useful task to make the game better.
Content_zh: 你得把所有回饋拿來，試著轉化成能讓遊戲變更好的實際工作。

Content: LeBreton: It sounds like partly you were just like, like when you're talking to the elder,
Content_zh: LeBreton：聽起來有一部分是，像你跟長老說話時，

Content: you were just kind of feeling some exposition fatigue, right?
Content_zh: 你有點覺得資訊說明太多，對吧？

Content: Like, Muir: Yes.
Content_zh: LeBreton：像是……Muir：對。

Content: LeBreton: he had lots to tell you and I don't know, spreading that out somehow or just...
Content_zh: LeBreton：他有很多事情要告訴你，我不知道，也許要把它分散一點，或只是……

Content: Muir: And it was like single world, single word questions that you're clicking on to like, for their progressed like,
Content_zh: Muir：而且那像是單字問題，你點那些詞，讓它們繼續推進，像是……

Content: LeBreton: Metal Gear. Muir: Yes!
Content_zh: LeBreton：Metal Gear。Muir：對！

Content: It totally reminded of Metal Gear.
Content_zh: 它完全讓我想到 Metal Gear。

Content: I don't want to say that.
Content_zh: 我本來不想說。

Content: Schafer: Well, we're going for a Metal Gear thing.
Content_zh: Schafer：嗯，我們就是要做出 Metal Gear 的感覺。

Content: LeBreton: Obviously.
Content_zh: LeBreton：顯然。

Content: Schafer: Who found the cardboard box?
Content_zh: Schafer：誰找到紙箱了？

Content: Schafer: That was great. That was pretty good.
Content_zh: Schafer：那很棒，真的不錯。

Content: Most of it was directed toward stuff that we just know we have to do anyway, that's already on the list to get done.
Content_zh: 大多數回饋都指向我們本來就知道要做的事，也已經在待辦清單上。

Content: Schafer: You've been through HOFs before.
Content_zh: Schafer：你以前參加過 HOF。

Content: Schafer: I was going to say, watching it through other people's eyes, I was really proud of how much work that you guys got done
Content_zh: Schafer：我想說，透過別人的眼睛來看，我真的很驕傲你們完成了這麼多工作。

Content: because it was like, the girl had an egg on her head and shoes on her feet and she was kicking up dust and she was climbing ladders.
Content_zh: 因為那女孩頭上有蛋、腳上有鞋，會踢起灰塵，也會爬梯子。

Content: And I was like, this game is way too complicated.
Content_zh: 我就想，這遊戲太複雜了。

Content: Who came up with all this stuff?
Content_zh: 這些東西到底誰想出來的？

Content: So we'll start thinking about a thread for all of this.
Content_zh: 所以我們會開始思考要怎麼替這些東西串出一條脈絡。

Content: Thanks for everyone hustling to get the build running.
Content_zh: 謝謝大家努力把 build 跑起來。

Content: It worked great.
Content_zh: 它跑得很好。

Content: I was very proud of it.
Content_zh: 我真的很以它為傲。

Content: It was awesome.
Content_zh: 超棒的。

Content: I hope no one's feelings got hurt.
Content_zh: 希望沒有人的感情受傷。

Content: Schafer: To some like, yeah, I do need to clarify the goals and the characters that's going to be hard to do.
Content_zh: Schafer：有些部分，對，我確實需要釐清目標和角色，那會很難做。

Content: Just more work for that one, but there's nothing that came up that was really terrifying,
Content_zh: 那只是需要更多工作，但沒有出現什麼真的可怕的問題。

Content: like oh God, how are we…
Content_zh: 不像那種，天啊，我們要怎麼……

Content: You know, when you're doing a game that has a lot of physical action and stuff in it,
Content_zh: 你知道，如果你做的是有很多物理動作之類的遊戲，

Content: sometimes people are like, it's just not, the bad, the swordplay's not fun, you know.
Content_zh: 有時候人們會說，這就是不行，劍術不好玩之類的。

Content: And then you're just like, oh, how are we going to solve that?
Content_zh: 然後你就會想，喔，這要怎麼解決？

Content: You know, it's just another iteration around.
Content_zh: 這只是再多一輪迭代。

Content: But I think everything, everything that was said could easily be turned into a task that we can use to make the game better.
Content_zh: 但我覺得大家說的每件事，都能很容易轉成讓遊戲更好的任務。

Content: So that's good.
Content_zh: 所以這很好。

Content: Schafer: I appreciate you guys playing it.
Content_zh: Schafer：謝謝你們玩它。

Content: Thank you for playing.
Content_zh: 謝謝你們來玩。

Content: Thank you for staying and chatting.
Content_zh: 謝謝你們留下來聊天。

Content: Thank you for clapping.
Content_zh: 謝謝你們鼓掌。

Content: [Clapping] Thanks for coming.
Content_zh: [掌聲] 謝謝大家來。

Content: Fill out your surveys.
Content_zh: 記得填問卷。

Content: Thank you.
Content_zh: 謝謝。

Content: [Music] Schafer: And some people were looking for a different experience.
Content_zh: [音樂] Schafer：有些人想要的是不同的體驗。

Content: Like you can tell some people were really into just progression and beating a game, which is not necessarily what a, I mean, adventure games are kind of about that,
Content_zh: 你看得出有些人很重視推進和破關。這不一定是……我是說，冒險遊戲某種程度上是那樣，

Content: but they're also for enjoying the ride.
Content_zh: 但它們也重視享受過程。

Content: So hopefully make it as enjoyable as possible.
Content_zh: 所以希望能讓這段過程盡可能好玩。

Content: Bye. Thanks for playing.
Content_zh: 掰，謝謝你們來玩。

Content: Muir: Hey, it's nice. I like it.
Content_zh: Muir：嘿，很不錯。我喜歡。

Content: It's really good.
Content_zh: 真的很好。

Content: Schafer: Awesome. Thanks for playing. Cool.
Content_zh: Schafer：太棒了，謝謝你來玩。酷。

Content: Muir: It's awesome. I'm excited.
Content_zh: Muir：很棒。我很期待。

Content: Schafer: That's nice.
Content_zh: Schafer：那很好。

Content: Brad.
Content_zh: Brad。

Content: He doesn't mean that.
Content_zh: 他不是認真的。

Content: He's going to say something really mean.
Content_zh: 他等一下會說很刻薄的話。

Content: It's not as good as Iron Brigade.
Content_zh: 它沒有《Iron Brigade》好。

Content: OK. Cool.
Content_zh: 好，酷。

Content: Awesome.
Content_zh: 太棒了。

Content: Just slap my face.
Content_zh: 直接打我臉吧。

Content: [Music]
Content_zh: [音樂]
